direction: Large-map side-scrolling street action inspired by flash-era district brawlers, with partner support, a layered weapon system, strong hit impact, and fluid combat.
visual_differentiation_axes: district warrant-board HUD instead of top stat pills or a wide bottom command strip
right-side partner pager as a dominant gameplay motif
asphalt navy and sodium amber palette instead of fantasy or neon-future defaults
evidence-wall navigation screens instead of plain centered button menus
taped fist plus cracked street-sign icon subject instead of shield tower zombie or helmet motifs
forbidden_visual_reuse: do not reuse a top-pill HUD with a full-width bottom action bar
do not reuse fantasy parchment, royal crest, or clean sci-fi glass panels
do not reuse post-apocalypse field-kit framing from survival shooters
do not reuse another project's exported icon silhouette or palette pairing
do not use a plain fist-only icon with no district-sign motif

ui_identity.layout_archetype: left dossier rail plus right partner pager plus lower-right combat slab and upper-left district stamp
ui_identity.hud_composition: vertical player status on the left, stacked partner portraits on the right, compact weapon and combo block at lower right, small block objective card at upper left
ui_identity.navigation_model: evidence-wall main menu to district board to route cards to combat sectors with safehouse drawers for crew and gear
ui_identity.palette_signature: asphalt navy, sodium amber, bruised crimson, faded police white, dirty brass, and cold mint route traces
ui_identity.material_language: scratched enamel signs, taped warrants, acetate evidence sleeves, chipped station metal, spray-painted route marks, and dented gear trays
ui_identity.typography_style: bold compressed action sans for titles, narrow dossier labels for system text, and thick impact numerals for combo and ammo
unique_screen_motifs: pinned mugshots on the crew screen
chalk route overlays on the district board
capture stamp graphics on result screens
confiscated weapon trays on the bench screen
forbidden_ui_elements: generic circular neon buttons as the main identity
plain centered three-button menu with no board motif
fantasy quest cards or royal banner framing

icon_identity.subject: taped fist raised in front of a cracked Old King district street sign with a short sawn-off silhouette at the base
icon_identity.silhouette: strong upright fist, broken rectangular sign plate, and short diagonal weapon line
icon_identity.composition: fist centered slightly left, cracked sign behind it, amber route slash crossing the sign, short weapon edge anchoring the lower third
icon_identity.palette: asphalt blue, sodium amber, dirty off-white sign paint, muted brass, and restrained crimson damage accents
icon_identity.background: dark enamel street-sign badge with edge wear, city haze, and light route scratches instead of a flat chip background
icon_identity.game_specific_motif: the broken district sign makes the icon about block takeover and urban territory fighting rather than generic action combat
forbidden_icon_reuse: no plain fist badge with no urban sign element
